title: NOC 20010 – Engineering Managers | GTR Immigration
description: Engineering Managers oversee complex technical operations, lead multidisciplinary engineering teams, and guide large-scale infrastructure, manufacturing, and research projects across public and private sectors in Canada. Classified under NOC 20010, this TEER 0 management occupation continues to experience strong national demand—creating premium immigration opportunities through employer-supported work permits, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), and long-term Permanent Residence (PR) strategies.

classification.category: 2 – Natural and applied sciences and related occupations
classification.major_group: 200 – Specialized middle management occupations in engineering, architecture, science, and information systems
classification.minor_group: 2001 – Managers in engineering, architecture, science, and information systems

common_job_titles: Director of engineering research and development
duties: Engineering Managers may perform some or all of the following:
Plan and oversee engineering department operations
Develop policies, technical standards, and procedures
Consult with clients and stakeholders on project specifications
Recruit staff and supervise professional development
Participate directly in technical design and inspections
employment_requirements: Registration as a Professional Engineer (P. Eng.) in a province or territory
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to create approximately 23,100 new positions for Engineering Managers, while about 28,800 job seekers are expected—making provincial nomination strategies, employer sponsorship, and executive-level immigration planning especially important for overseas professionals.
additional_information: Engineering Managers remain in demand across infrastructure development, energy, telecommunications, manufacturing, and advanced research sectors nationwide. Hiring has accelerated since the pandemic and is expected to remain strong in the coming years.
Engineering consulting firmsManufacturing and construction companiesEnergy and utilities providersTechnology enterprisesGovernment departments and agenciesScientific research institutions
Expansion of infrastructure and clean-energy projectsDigital-transformation initiativesIndustrial modernizationSenior-level leadership requirementsEmployer-driven recruitment
Annual salaries typically range between $76,000 and $166,600 CAD, depending on province, specialization, and leadership experience.
